title: What I think is missing from Rebuild
display_title: What I think is missing from Rebuild
wikitext:
[]: What I think is missing from Rebuild...             Quote Post by chaosakita » Tue Jun 19, 2012 1:59 am I really love Rebuild, and I can't wait until the next movie comes out. For me, I feel that even if it's not quite as good as the original, I think that it stands out as its own work. However, I feel like the thing that's missing from Rebuild is the music from the original series. The new soundtrack is alright, but I think that Eva isn't quite the same without the great pieces like "Thanatos" (which was used once), "Borderline Case", and "Mother is the First Other." I feel like it'd also be a great callback to the original series if the songs could be re-used. Are you guys happy with the new soundtrack or would you like more songs from the original soundtrack?

[]:             Quote Post by Brainman » Tue Jun 19, 2012 2:08 am The music was fine. I liked the part with the trumpets in the classroom scene. But I will say, while dissonant music set to crazy or tragic imagery is something I generally like, they shouldn't have done it twice in the same stupid movie. It's a diminishing returns thing, even if they felt the individual scenes called for it. For the next one I'm hoping for a lot of classical tunes now that Karwou is in the mix.

[]:             Quote Post by Na7e » Tue Jun 19, 2012 2:41 am Brainman wrote: But I will say, while dissonant music set to crazy or tragic imagery is something I generally like, they shouldn't have done it twice in the same stupid movie. It's a diminishing returns thing, even if they felt the individual scenes called for it.

[]: What Na7e said.             Quote Post by Mori-kun » Tue Jun 19, 2012 2:54 pm IMO, NME will (not) live up to the standards of character development that NGE set, so I don't see why the lack of Shinji+Asuka interaction should be referred to as a "deficiency". I also don't believe that they were trying to garner sympathy for Shinji's feelings toward Asuka in that scene with "Kyou", because a song as sombre as that is only fitting for an instance that draws unnerving parallels to the rising action of EoE, as well as having a childish tone which contrasts the inter-Eva brutality of it all. I could be wrong, though, since I haven't read the storyboards or the producers' thoughts about this scene.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jun 19, 2012 3:21 pm The "Thanatos" theme is used in 2.XX when Eva-00 gets eaten and Shinji runs to the Cage (2EM34, "Evanescence - mouvement 2").

[]:             Quote Post by Ceimoa Nan » Tue Jun 19, 2012 4:47 pm Actually, I think my biggest problem with NME is also the music. But I just don't like that English chanting Sagisu uses.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Jun 19, 2012 4:55 pm I didn't see the musical cognitive dissonance used twice in one film as too much of a stretch. The bombastic action building up to its second use was, in my opinion, enough to set one up for that again. And after all, that last scene when through 12 different emotions anyway (give or take a few), so it was okay that one of those emotions bashing at the audience was one that they've already experienced previously in the same movie. But that's just my opinion. In order for 2.22 to use "Mother is the First Other," they would need to go into Shinji's mother a bit more. And it would have been really awkward if the musical theme just popped up in either of the two scenes Yui was mentioned. Maybe 1.11 could have used the tracks somewhere, seeing as how that film alone allows the NME films to have more Hell Train Scenes that all of episodes 1-19 of NGE, but the silence works really well as the films are simply introducing the viewer to both the mental environment and the ideas being discussed. We haven't heard Shinji complain about nearly as much issues in the first part of NME as he did in all off NGE. But if Anno and Sagisu are still interested in reusing more music from NGE, the end of 2.22 was set up perfectly for both "Borderline Case" and "Mother is the First Other" to be revisited with a full orchestra in 3.0. Think about it, Shinji's currently stuck with both Rei and Yui inside the shell of Unit 01. This just begs to bring the viewer into Freudian psychological weirdness. So for right now, I've always felt what was missing from NME were the last two movies. My only complaints about the first two films so far is how rushed 2.22 can feel with Asuka from time to time. But even in its quickened pacing, I can't say any introductory elements about the character are missing. After the new series is complete, then I'll nit-pick about what may or may not be missing.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue Jun 19, 2012 7:43 pm What's missing from Rebuild is Parts 3 & 4.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Jun 19, 2012 8:20 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: What's missing from Rebuild is Parts 3 & 4.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jun 19, 2012 9:22 pm I still wouldn't mind if some of the unused S2 Works tracks make their way in 3.0 or 4.0, but hopefully the music in either of the next two films (whether reworking pieces from NGE or new BGM) will be on par with EoE's soundtrack. I also agree that 3.0 and 4.0 need to be released before fans can make a judgement call on the story as a whole.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Wed Jun 20, 2012 3:12 am I really don't understand the criticism that "Give Me Wings" is counter-intuitive in the same way as "Today is a time for Goodbye". "Goodbye"'s beauty is contrasted against horrific imagery. "Wings" is majestic music for majestic imagery -- the songs aren't used in the same way at all. Drums and electric guitars for the closing minutes would have been inappropriate.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jun 20, 2012 3:46 am Warren Peace wrote: "Wings" is majestic music for majestic imagery

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Wed Jun 20, 2012 4:57 am There are moments of horror to be sure, but the thrust of the scene is very much focused on Shinji's triumph in rescuing Rei. The music reflects that struggle more than anything else, swelling when he finally reaches her. That's very different from the ironic use displayed earlier with "Goodbye" -- the final segment is in sync with the imagery on display, not counterbalancing it. I say all this without making judgments about the worth of Shinji's actions, btw -- EoE's Third Impact is presented in a similarly majestic (yes) manner, even though that's proven to be a bad thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jun 20, 2012 5:34 am Warren Peace wrote: the final segment is in sync with the imagery on display, not counterbalancing it.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Wed Jun 20, 2012 6:10 am Well, to Shinji, it is a celebratory tone. And because the scene operates primarily from his point of view, the way events are portrayed reflects this. Again, it's completely separate from the righteousness of his actions -- it's a matter of perspective, not morals. The film wants to get across what Shinji is feeling. "Wings" operates as hero music because Shinji thinks he is one. The film drops hints (like Ritsuko) that he probably isn't. Still, it's all about what Shinji feels at that moment. You're looking at things from a macro perspective ("Shinji is a selfish dick who will destroy the world") when the film is mostly micro ("Shinji is a brave guy who will save his friend"). The film mixes in some macro via Ritsuko because they want us skeptical of the micro, but the music is on micro's side. Er, so to speak.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jun 20, 2012 12:42 pm Warren: If all you want to say is that the music fits from Shinji's perspective, okay, yeah, that works. But is it dissonant from the audience perspective? Absolutely. The heated threads about the ending and Shinji's characterization make it pretty obvious that the effect of the finale is keenly individual. The scene is clearly designed to produce a mixed effect. If you first-and-foremost feel the triumphant and sentimental aspects despite the ominous and apocalyptic ones, then, as you say, the music might feel synchronous. If it's the inverse, or right on the fence, or outright bafflement, then the commonly reported feeling of musical dissonance is probably in effect. For the record, I was assessing the 'dissonance factor' purely from the perspective of contrasting overall visual and narrative elements with the musical one, and putting a broader 'audience POV' over the clusterfuck that is 'Shinji POV'. The latter is much narrower than the former; the audience, not Shinji, is reacting to the deliberately chosen images, dialogue, and music; etc.

[]:             Quote Post by gorgeousshutin » Thu Jun 21, 2012 3:37 pm What's missing is the TIME to get the original characters properly fleshed out, which leave them all much more 2D than in the original work. I can't believe that given the time constraints of the movies, Anno'd still see fit to include some self-insert-lite OC just to take up film-space. If not for my love for the original series, there's no way I'd still be following Rebuilt.

[]:             Quote Post by driftking18594 » Fri Jun 22, 2012 4:13 am I think we're going to see some significant character development for Asuka and Mari in 3.0. Besides, it's going to be impossible for Anno to explain away Asuka hopping straight back into an Eva minutes after regaining consciousness, completely forgetting what's happened to her.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Jun 30, 2012 9:42 am I didn't see the Tsubasa-Kudasai-Scene as dissonace - there was a new version of the Beast II playing for most of the *actual* fighting and stomping and transforming, it was only after zeruel was thoroughly pwned that we got to hear Tsubasa kudasai. It's just that the American fandom... *exclusively* the American fandom, as far as I have heard, not the Japanese one, sees the need to twist a scene into the blackest pits of negativity that was discribed by those who thought it up as being about "salvation". I think "salvation" goes very well with piano music about freedom. Personally, I thought the music was one of the *best* things about the movie and that it reached a nice balance between rehearsed old stuff and epic new stuff... I found the "english chanting" song particularly awesome, but meh, personal taste I guess. Still, wouldn't the composer just get *incredibly bored* from doing the same thing all over again?

[]:             Quote Post by Brainman » Sat Jun 30, 2012 12:44 pm If their intent was to make the scene about salvation without any irony or other side to it, then they wouldn't have been yapping about the world being about to end as a result of Shinji's freedom. What are we supposed to do, just ignore Ritsuko or not take it seriously when Misato almost panics when the wings appear?

However, I feel like the thing that's missing from Rebuild is the music from the original series. The new soundtrack is alright, but I think
